code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import numpy as np
    import pandas as pd

    short = params['ema_short']
    long = params['ema_long']

    # Compute EMAs
    ema_short = df['close'].ewm(span=short, adjust=False).mean()
    ema_long = df['close'].ewm(span=long, adjust=False).mean()

    # Compute ADX
    high = df['high']
    low = df['low']
    close = df['close']
    tr = np.maximum(high - low, np.maximum(abs(high - close.shift(1)), abs(low - close.shift(1))))
    atr = tr.rolling(14).mean()
    # Directional movement
    up_move = high - high.shift(1)
    down_move = low.shift(1) - low
    plus_dm = np.where((up_move > down_move) & (up_move > 0), up_move, 0)
    minus_dm = np.where((down_move > up_move) & (down_move > 0), down_move, 0)
    plus_di = 100 * pd.Series(plus_dm).rolling(14).mean() / atr
    minus_di = 100 * pd.Series(minus_dm).rolling(14).mean() / atr
    dx = 100 * abs(plus_di - minus_di) / (plus_di + minus_di + 1e-10)
    adx = dx.rolling(14).mean()

    # Volatility filter: ATR above median of last 50
    atr_median = atr.rolling(50).median()
    volatility_ok = atr > atr_median

    # Trend signal
    trend_up = (ema_short > ema_long) & (adx > 25)
    trend_down = (ema_short < ema_long) & (adx > 25)

    # Entry signals only when volatility is high
    long_entry = trend_up & volatility_ok
    short_entry = trend_down & volatility_ok

    # Generate positions: 1 for long, -1 for short, 0 for flat
    position = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    position[long_entry] = 1
    position[short_entry] = -1

    # Exit when trend weakens or reverses
    for i in range(1, len(position)):
        if position.iloc[i-1] == 1:
            if (ema_short.iloc[i] < ema_long.iloc[i]) or (adx.iloc[i] < 20):
                position.iloc[i] = 0
            else:
                position.iloc[i] = 1
        elif position.iloc[i-1] == -1:
            if (ema_short.iloc[i] > ema_long.iloc[i]) or (adx.iloc[i] < 20):
                position.iloc[i] = 0
            else:
                position.iloc[i] = -1

    return position
rationale: USD/CHF on H4: during periods of above-average volatility, trend-following signals from moving average crossovers are more reliable as they capture directional moves driven by shifts in monetary policy expectations.

self_critique.why_this_might_be_noise: Moving average crossovers are widely used and may be overfitted; the volatility filter introduces a look-ahead bias if not computed strictly on past data (but here it is).
self_critique.what_would_disprove_this: If the strategy fails in choppy markets where volatility is high but direction is random, or during prolonged low-volatility trends.
